extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the sprint after echelon racing in crosswinds shredded the peloton and significantly reduced the breakaway's advantage mid-stage
tactical_patterns: Crosswind echelons in countryside systematically destroyed breakaway advantage from 3:45 down, revealing UAE/Ineos wind vigilance protected GC.
MORKOV Michael delivered BENNETT perfectly inside 200m twice this race, establishing Deceuninck sprint train dominance pattern.
ACKERMANN Pascal caught behind echelons required significant energy chase, compromising his sprint finish to only third despite regaining contact.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Two-from-two in sprint stages suggests superior form and positioning over higher-ranked sprinters like Ewan
rider_signals.DEKKER David: neutral: Fourth place but gained green jersey on points, indicating consistent top-5 finishes trump outright wins
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Zero wins despite being primary threat suggests either team leadout failures or positioning errors in crucial final kilometer
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Caught behind crosswinds and requiring chase effort reveals vulnerability to echelon racing, compromising sprint legs
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: UAE Emirates actively pacemaking in crosswinds on flat sprint stage shows unusual GC protection vigilance with 45-second lead
